---
title: "RNA-seq Analysis of Dilated Cardiomyopathy (DCM)"
---

## Project Overview

This project analyzes gene expression differences in dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) using RNA-seq data, with a focus on sex-specific effects.

## Introduction

Dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) is a condition where the heart becomes enlarged and weakened, reducing its ability to pump blood effectively. This can lead to heart failure, arrhythmias, and sudden cardiac death in severe cases.

This project uses RNA-seq data to study gene expression changes in DCM compared to non-failing hearts and to determine whether these changes differ between males and females.

## Research Question

Which genes and molecular pathways differ between males and females with dilated cardiomyopathy compared to non-failing hearts?

## Hypothesis

DCM hearts show distinct gene expression and pathway changes compared to non-failing hearts, with stronger inflammatory and remodeling signals. These effects are expected to differ between males and females.

## Dataset

- GSE141910
- Total samples: 305
- Non-failing: 152
  - 70 males
  - 82 females
- DCM: 153
  - 92 males
  - 61 females

## Key Findings

- DCM samples separate from controls in PCA and UMAP
- Gene expression differences are present between male and female DCM hearts
- Inflammation and metabolism-related pathways are important
- Disease effects are stronger than sex effects
